regimeNotes: NEUTRAL regime maintained 2026-07-04 (Saturday; US equity markets closed for the weekend -- SPY/MSFT/GOOGL/UNH/NVDA/GLD marks all unchanged from the July 2 close, confirmed via stockanalysis.com fallback since Stooq CSV endpoint was unreachable/404 today). Crypto trades 24/7 and ticked up modestly: BTC $62,556 (+0.8%), ETH $1,763.11 (+1.4%), SOL $81.38 (-0.2%); total crypto mcap $2.26T (+1.07% 24h), BTC dominance 55.60% (stable), Fear & Greed 22 Extreme Fear (vs 21 yesterday, essentially unchanged). Macro backdrop unchanged from the prior read: soft June NFP (+57K vs ~110K consensus) cooling near-term hike odds, DXY ~100.86, 10Y yield ~4.485%, gold holding its extended rally. No FOMC or data catalysts over the weekend. Crypto sleeve now 19.80% of NAV (drifted up slightly on crypto's weekend move), still just under the 20% neutral cap -- no room to add without breaching the guardrail. Cash 13.61% of NAV, comfortably above the 5% floor. MSFT sits at 14.99% of NAV, effectively at its 15% entry cap. NAV 99,173.59 (+0.17% vs July 3). REGIME UNCHANGED (neutral): with equities closed for the weekend and crypto's move immaterial, there is no new evidence to revisit any thesis or invalidation level. No invalidation levels breached; no guardrail breaches. HOLD day, zero trades.

SPY.thesis: Core US equity beta at $744.78 (above $741.75 avgCost; +0.41%, ~13.16% NAV). Markets closed for the weekend; mark unchanged from July 2 close. Broad index still the diversified anchor while breadth stays uneven under the surface (chip/megacap tech softness). Hold.
SPY.invalidationLevel: Sustained close below 660 USD or confirmed multi-hike path forcing a technical downtrend in the broad index.
